briefs.title: Disaster Financial Assistance Claims Now Require Full Documentation Upfront in BC
briefs.summary: BC updated its disaster financial assistance rules so that businesses and residents must now submit a complete application — including all invoices and supporting documents — when first applying for help after a disaster. The Provincial Emergency Program will review eligibility before sending the file to an adjuster (an independent person who calculates how much money you're owed), and the final decision on payment now rests with the Program, not the adjuster.
briefs.action_required: If you apply for disaster financial assistance, gather all invoices and supporting documents before submitting your application.

key_dates.label: BC Disaster Financial Assistance amended application rules take effect
